fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 73 is inverted: clamping is applied when `clamp` is False instead of when `clamp` is True, causing extrapolated values to be unclamped when clamping is requested and clamped when it is not.

constraints.intent: When clamp is True, the branch body (clamping logic) should be entered — the condition `not clamp` is inverted, so when clamp=True the clamping block is skipped.
The then-branch should only be taken when clamp is False; if it is taken when clamp is True the condition is inverted.

spec.expr: (not clamp) or (y__5 >= min(y0, y1) and y__5 <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: When clamp is True, the final y value must lie within the anchor probability range.

spec.expr: (not clamp) or (y__5 >= min(y0, y1) and y__5 <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: Before returning, if clamping was requested the interpolated value must be bounded by the anchor range.

spec.expr: (not clamp) or (__cbfl_result >= min(y0, y1) and __cbfl_result <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: The returned probability must be within the anchor range when clamping is enabled.
